note: source.url https://www.cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-resources/products/approved-inverters returns HTTP 404. The correct current URL is https://cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-programs/products-program/inverters (confirmed live, 1800+ approved models listed). CEC approved-inverters list exists and is official. AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 prerequisite for CEC listing is confirmed correct. mandatory_status 'de facto mandatory for grid-tied residential/commercial PV sales' is substantively accurate. URL should be corrected.

source.url https://www.cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-resources/products/approved-inverters returns HTTP 404. Correct URL is https://cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-programs/products-program/inverters. Standard AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 confirmed as current and mandatory — note: Amendment 2:2024 came into effect 23 August 2025, introducing additional region-settings requirements; fragment text should ideally reference AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Amd 2:2024 for post-Aug-2025 compliance. Also note AS/NZS 4777.1:2016 referenced in standards_or_laws has been superseded by AS/NZS 4777.1:2024 (mandatory from 23 February 2025). Content on DRED, test lab, and CEC listing pathway is substantively correct. URL must be fixed.

source.url https://www.standards.org.au/standards-catalogue/sa-snz/electrotechnology/el-042/as-nzs--4777-dot-2-2020 returns HTTP 404. The correct Standards Australia catalogue URL for this standard uses the 'other' sub-path: https://www.standards.org.au/standards-catalogue/sa-snz/other/el-042/as-slash-nzs--4777-dot-2-colon-2020, or preferably the canonical query-form: https://www.standards.org.au/standards-catalogue/standard-details?designation=as-nzs-4777-2-2020. DRM0–DRM5 scope and 2-second response requirement confirmed correct per AEMO and CEC sources. Mandatory status confirmed. AS/NZS 4777.1 reference should be updated to 2024 edition. DRED connector content is technically accurate per industry sources.

source.url https://www.cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry/products/inverters returns HTTP 404. Correct URL is https://cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-programs/products-program/inverters (confirmed live). Region A/B/C/NZ framework, volt-watt and volt-var default-on, and parameter-locking requirements are confirmed correct under AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 and its Amd 2:2024. Note: Amd 2:2024 strengthened region-settings compliance; CEC removed non-compliant models from the approved list from August 2025. Fragment's Region B description ('Queensland and SA') may need review against the standard — the search evidence found Region B described as 'SA' only in some sources; verify clauses 2.3/Table 3.1-3.4 directly for B/C exact geographic scope.

source.url https://www.acma.gov.au/compliance-and-enforcement/electromagnetic-compatibility timed out on fetch; however the canonical ACMA EMC supplier path is confirmed at https://acma.gov.au/Industry/Suppliers/Regulatory-arrangements/EMC-Electromagnetic-compatibility and the ACMA EMC framework is official and active. Standard citation 'AS/NZS CISPR 11 ' — verification found that AS/NZS CISPR 11:2024 is now the current edition (adopted from CISPR 11:2024, confirmed via Standards NZ and Standards Australia search). Fragment references only 'AS/NZS CISPR 11' without edition, which is acceptable since the fragment already flags it as UNVERIFIED. Mandatory status (RCM under Radiocommunications Act 1992) is confirmed correct. Note: ACMA search results confirm solar inverter suppliers must comply with CISPR 11 and be labelled with RCM — this aligns with fragment content. ACMA URL appears to have restructured since original authoring; the live equivalent is at https://www.acma.gov.au/step-1-check-rules-follow for the 5-step supplier process.

source.url https://www.acma.gov.au/compliance-and-enforcement/electromagnetic-compatibility timed out on direct fetch; the ACMA EMC framework is confirmed active and official via search. AS/NZS 61000-6-2 adoption status is marked UNVERIFIED in fragment — this is the correct flag since no direct confirmation of the exact current AS/NZS edition was found in search. IEC 61000-6-2 heritage and technical alignment with GB/T 17799.2 is well-established. Mandatory status confirmed. ACMA URL may have changed — https://www.acma.gov.au/step-2-show-your-product-complies is the current equivalent for compliance evidence guidance. Retains needs_human because AS/NZS 61000-6-2 specific edition adoption by ACMA was not directly confirmed via official source in this check.

source.url https://www.cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry/products/inverters returns HTTP 404. Correct URL is https://cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-programs/products-program/inverters (confirmed live). AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 as mandatory grid-connection standard confirmed; note: AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Amd 2:2024 in effect from 23 August 2025. CN standards (GB/T 19964, NB/T 32004) not recognised by CEC or DNSPs — confirmed correct. Note: GB/T 19964 entry in cn_common_equivalent cites 2012 version but fragment text in aupv-drm-001 cites the updated GB/T 19964-2024; 2012 year in this row is slightly stale but not a hard error for informational purposes.

source.url https://www.cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry/products/inverters returns HTTP 404. Correct URL is https://cleanenergycouncil.org.au/industry-programs/products-program/inverters. Region A/B/C descriptions in fragment: 'Region A — predominantly south-east Australia; Region B — Queensland and SA; Region C — WA and remote/off-grid-capable networks' — this differs from the standard's actual definitions. Verified sources describe Region B as primarily SA (South Australia), not 'Queensland and SA'; Region A covers south-east Australia including Queensland's interconnected grid. This is a factual error in the region description. AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Amd 2:2024 region-settings compliance confirmed mandatory from August 2025.

source.url https://www.aemo.com.au/energy-systems/electricity/national-electricity-market-nem/participate-in-the-market/generators-and-schedulers returns HTTP 403 and is the wrong page — it covers generator market registration for large schedulable plant, not DRM/inverter grid-support requirements for distributed PV. The correct AEMO source for this topic is https://www.aemo.com.au/initiatives/major-programs/nem-distributed-energy-resources-der-program/standards-and-connections/as-nzs-4777-2-inverter-requirements-standard (confirmed in search, covers AS/NZS 4777.2, DRM, volt-var, volt-watt for DER). Standard content and mandatory status are correct. DRM0–DRM5/DRM8 requirements, volt-watt and volt-var default-on confirmed via AEMO DER program sources.

source.url https://standards.org.au/standards-catalogue/sa-snz/electrotechnology/el-042/as-nzs-4777-dot-2-2020 returns HTTP 404. Note the path variant here uses 'electrotechnology' (not 'other') and drops double-hyphens — both cause 404. Correct Standards Australia URL is https://www.standards.org.au/standards-catalogue/standard-details?designation=as-nzs-4777-2-2020. Also note: AS/NZS 4777.1:2016 referenced in standards_or_laws has been superseded by AS/NZS 4777.1:2024 (mandatory from 23 February 2025). Anti-islanding and DNSP approval content is substantively correct.

source.url https://www.acma.gov.au/industry-library/industry/guides-and-research/rcm-regulatory-compliance-mark timed out on fetch and appears to be a stale/restructured URL. Current ACMA RCM guidance is accessible via https://www.acma.gov.au/step-5-label-your-product and https://www.eess.gov.au/rcm/regulatory-compliance-mark-rcm-general/ (both confirmed live in search). EESS framework, RCM replacing A-Tick/C-Tick, AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 as applicable standard, and Responsible Supplier obligations are all confirmed correct. Note: fragment's UNVERIFIED caveat about 2025–2026 standard revision is well-placed — AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Amd 2:2024 came into effect 23 August 2025, which represents a significant update. The mandatory status description is accurate.

source.url https://www.erac.gov.au/electrical-equipment-safety-system returns HTTP 404. The EESS has migrated to a dedicated site: https://www.eess.gov.au (confirmed live). Responsible Supplier registration is now at https://www.eess.gov.au/registration/registration-responsible-supplier/ (confirmed live — EESS Platform replaced the old database in October 2024). Fragment content on ABN requirement, model-level registration, and the absence of a CN equivalent is confirmed correct. Note: eess.gov.au is the current authoritative URL, not erac.gov.au sub-paths for EESS content.

source.url https://www.erac.gov.au/electrical-equipment-safety-system/equipment-levels returns HTTP 404. Current URL for EESS Level 3 information is https://www.eess.gov.au/registration/registration-in-scope-electrical-equipment/level-3/ (confirmed live). Level 3 equipment requiring third-party test and RCM confirmed correct. Fragment's UNVERIFIED caveat on PV inverter classification level is well-placed — the eess.gov.au Level 3 page does not explicitly name PV inverters; classification should be verified against current EESS schedule. AS/NZS 4777.1:2016 reference in standards_or_laws is stale — superseded by AS/NZS 4777.1:2024 mandatory from 23 February 2025. AEMO reference document '' is appropriate.

source.url https://www.erac.gov.au/electrical-equipment-safety-system/ returns HTTP 404. Current authoritative URL is https://www.eess.gov.au (confirmed). AS/NZS 62109-1:2011 and AS/NZS 62109-2:2011 as mandatory safety standards for PV inverters under EESS confirmed — eess.gov.au Level 3 page confirms third-party certification required for Level 3 equipment. Fragment's UNVERIFIED note about IEC 62109-1:2022/62109-2:2022 amendment adoption is appropriate. CB Scheme pathway for IEC 62109-1/-2 as supporting evidence (with EESS registration still required) is confirmed correct. Mandatory status is accurate.

source.url https://www.erac.gov.au/electrical-equipment-safety-system/responsible-suppliers/ returns HTTP 404. Current URL is https://www.eess.gov.au/registration/registration-responsible-supplier/ (confirmed live). RCM as mandatory conformity mark combining former A-tick and C-tick is confirmed correct. Self-declaration system with compliance evidence held on file is confirmed. EESS Platform migration completed October 2024. EMC standard reference 'AS/NZS 4268' in the target_requirement.summary is potentially incorrect for PV inverters — AS/NZS 4268 covers wireless LAN; the correct EMC standard for PV inverters is AS/NZS CISPR 11 (confirmed by ACMA sources). This may be a drafting error in the fragment.

source.url https://www.erac.gov.au/electrical-equipment-safety-system/responsible-suppliers/how-to-register/ returns HTTP 404. Current URL is https://www.eess.gov.au/registration/registration-responsible-supplier/ (confirmed live). ABN requirement for Responsible Supplier confirmed. EESS Platform replaced old registration database in October 2024 — the erac.gov.au sub-paths for EESS are no longer valid. IECEE CB Scheme as supporting (not substituting) evidence confirmed correct. Analogy to UK SI 2016/1101 Authorised Representative is conceptually sound. Mandatory status is accurate.
